eventName: Getting Started with AI Agents: From Concepts to Code
description: A beginner-friendly evening of talks covering what AI agents are, how they work under the hood, and how to design them for real users. You'll leave understanding tool use, agent loops, and human-in-the-loop patterns — no PhD required.
targetAudience: Developers curious about AI agents, from beginners to intermediate builders

suggestedSpeakers.name: Marcus Johnson
suggestedSpeakers.reason: AI agents and tool use are his core topics. His DevRel background and 4.9 rating make him ideal for making agent concepts accessible to beginners.

suggestedSpeakers.name: Elena Vasquez
suggestedSpeakers.reason: Her expertise in human-in-the-loop and AI product design provides the crucial 'how to build agents people actually want to use' perspective.

suggestedVenue.name: Downtown Tech Hub
suggestedVenue.reason: Capacity of 80 handles the 50-attendee estimate with headroom. Projector, mic system, and central location make it ideal for a talk-format event.

slots.title: What Are AI Agents? Demystifying the Buzzword
slots.speaker: marcus-johnson

slots.title: Tool Use & Function Calling: How Agents Actually Work
slots.speaker: marcus-johnson

slots.title: Designing Agents for Real Users: Human-in-the-Loop Patterns
slots.speaker: elena-vasquez

slots.title: Lightning Round: 3 Agent Architectures in 15 Minutes
slots.speaker: marcus-johnson
